message.text: SQL injection in document search endpoint. The query parameter 'q' is interpolated directly into a SQL LIKE clause via f-string: `f"SELECT * FROM documents WHERE title LIKE '%{query}%'"`. An attacker can break out of the LIKE pattern to execute arbitrary SQL — e.g., `%' UNION SELECT username, password, '', '' FROM users --` to exfiltrate credentials, or `%'; DROP TABLE documents; --` for destructive attacks. Use parameterized queries: `cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM documents WHERE title LIKE ?", (f'%{query}%',))`.

Insecure Direct Object Reference in document search. The user_id parameter is taken directly from the client-supplied query string with no authorization check to verify the requesting user matches the target user_id. An attacker can enumerate or supply arbitrary user_id values to access other users' documents. The server must derive user_id from the authenticated session rather than accepting it as client input.

The GET /api/documents/search endpoint requires no authentication. Any unauthenticated user can search documents belonging to any user by supplying an arbitrary user_id. This compounds the IDOR vulnerability — without authentication there is no identity to authorize against. Add authentication middleware (e.g., session validation or token verification) and derive the user_id from the authenticated principal.
